title: How AI is reshaping global newsrooms: What Bangladesh journalists can do
description: Global mainstream media organizations like Associated Press, BBC, Wall Street Journal are increasingly integrating artificial intelligence into their daily workflows to increase productivity of journalists, optimize news production, automate repetitive tasks, and execute complex investigations.However in Bangladesh, there is a significant gap in terms of institutional formalization and less focus on how we can use available free tools in newsrooms to increase our productivity.In this article we will compare our local newsrooms with the global context. We also have some tips on how we can customize free AI tools until our newsrooms adopt their tailored AI models as per editorial

title: Samsung's next-generation AI memory chip sales top $1B after launch: Report
description: South Korean chipmaker expected to see HBM4 revenue exceed $1.2B by end-June, according to Yonhap

title: XRP x402 Kit Targets a 120 Million-Transaction AI Market as Ruvi (RUVI) Pays Trainers at $0.028 Entry
description: Ripple released its x402 XRPL AI Starter Kit on June 11, 2026, positioning the XRP Ledger against a broader x402 market that has already processed over 120 million transactions across 14 blockchains, counting on its 3-to-5-second settlement and built-in DEX

title: Japanese company Sakana whose Fugu AI model matched Claude Fable 5’s performance shares a message for all countries banned from using Anthropic's most powerful AI models
description: Japan's Sakana AI has launched Fugu, a multi-model orchestration system that matches Anthropic's export-restricted Claude Fable 5 and Mythos on benchmarks—without using either. Fugu pools public models like Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.5, and Claude Opus 4.8, picking and combining them per task through one API. CEO David Ha pitches it as a hedge against single-vendor dependence after US export controls cut off Anthropic's top models. Critics call it a closed orchestrator on closed models.

title: Rivian launched the most advanced AI assistant for drivers — much of the system is built by the Rivian and Volkswagen Group team from Belgrade: "Edge is where things get very complex"
description: Rivian's Assistant is built into the car's software and hardware, controls core vehicle functions, and runs partly on-device — and a Belgrade team builds the edge side. The AI era is marked by big transformation on one side and a lot of window dressing on the other. There aren't many products that [...]
